{{Quote|Out of our way, citizen. We're on official Sith business. Just stand aside if you know what's good for you!|A Sith trooper|<ref name="KOTOR" />}}
'''Sith Troopers''', also known as '''Sith Legionnaires''' or '''Sith Soldiers''', were [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] units in the [[Sith military (Jedi Civil War)|armed forces]] of the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]].

==History==
The [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] troopers, also known as Sith Legionnaires<ref name="KOTOR" /> or Sith Soldiers,<ref name="GoH">''[[Star Wars: Galaxy of Heroes]]''</ref> were the [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] units of the [[Sith military (Jedi Civil War)|Sith military]] of the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. The majority of troopers of [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] returning armada were traitorous members of the [[Republic Army]], who were later joined by idealistic volunteers from conquered [[Planet/Legends|planets]]. The weak were siphoned out through the merciless Sith training schemes, resulting in an efficient and powerful fighting force. The fear present in each trooper not only spurred them on in battle, but also prevented them from turning traitor for a second time.<ref name="KOTOR" />

Some Sith troopers used the fear they instilled in people and the influence they gained from the suit for their own gain, threatening people and intimidating citizens into giving them what they wanted. Others, however, saw themselves as simply doing their job, or believed that the Sith would be better at ruling [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

Sith troopers were often seen as expendable by their superiors, much like [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. For example, when [[Darth Malak]] was warned by [[Saul Karath]] that [[Destruction of Taris|bombing]] the planet of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] in order to kill [[Bastila Shan]] would result in the deaths of thousands of the Sith's own soldiers, he merely warned Karath against the perils of questioning his orders.<ref name="KOTOR" />

Following the [[Jedi Civil War]], many of the then-demoralized Sith troopers came under the command of the newly formed [[Sith Triumvirate]] ruled by [[Kreia|Darth Traya]], [[Darth Nihilus]], and [[Darth Sion]]. At that time, they participated in the [[Battle of Onderon (Onderon Civil War)|Second Battle of Onderon]] and the [[Battle of Telos IV]].<ref name="KOTOR II" />

==Armor==
{{Main|Sith trooper armor/Legends}}
The standard armor and helmet of a Sith trooper was colored either reflective [[Silver/Legends|silver]] or [[Color/Legends|white]] with [[Color/Legends|black]] under armor padding as well as a visor covering the wearers face. There was no visible indication of rank or division, although Sith Elite troops and Sith Commandos wore red and/or copper armor for distinction.<ref name="KOTOR II" />

Sith trooper armor, while bearing the same level of functionality of most other types of armor, was designed specifically to induce fear in the opponent. The armor casing was emphasized, while the trooper was completely obscured from view by a one-way visor, creating the impression of a faceless enemy.<ref name="KOTOR II" /> A similar effect would be recreated thousands of years later in the Republic [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]],<ref name="Episode II">{{Film|II}}</ref> who, through a fitting twist of fate, later served as the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], another empire ruled by the Sith.<ref name="Episode III">{{Film|III}}</ref>

While stranded on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] during Sith occupation, a mind-wiped [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Carth Onasi]] stole a suit of Sith armor, in order to enter the [[Lower City]] and continue their search for [[Bastila Shan]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

==Variants==
'''Sith assault troopers''' wore silver-gold armor and were each armed with a blaster rifle and a sword. They attempted to board the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' at the [[Peragus Mining Facility]].<ref name="KOTOR II" />

[[File:SithCommando.jpg|thumb|right|115px|A Sith [[Commando/Legends|Commando]]]]
'''Sith commandos''' were elite Sith soldiers, but how they differed in combat role from the standard troopers is unknown. They usually wore scarlet red armor and were issued [[Light repeating blaster/Legends|light repeating blasters]]. Commandos seen at the Battle of Telos IV during the [[First Jedi Purge]] wore silver-colored armor. During the purge, the Sith Triumvirate deployed commandos on [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], [[Citadel Station]] in orbit above [[Telos IV]], the [[Ravager (Centurion-class)|''Ravager'']], and at the [[Trayus Academy]] on [[Malachor V]].<ref name="KOTOR II" />

'''Sith elite gunners''' wore copper-colored battle suits. They were often armed with [[Disruptor rifle/Legends|disruptor rifles]], which were capable of penetrating personal [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]].

'''Sith heavy troopers''' were rarely seen heavy-weapon specialists employed by the Sith. They also wore red armor, and they were armed with [[Heavy repeating blaster/Legends|Heavy repeating blasters]]. Heavy troopers were sighted only in areas of great importance to the Sith war machine, which would seem to imply that their rank was higher than that of standard Sith troopers, or even Sith elite gunners. Revan fought a host of these soldiers during his assault on the [[Star Forge]] during the [[Battle of Rakata Prime]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

'''Sith grenadiers''' used [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]], mostly fragmentation or concussion types. They also wielded [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]]. They were also known to use [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]] in battle.

'''Sith wranglers''' used tamed beasts in combat. They were employed in battles where a significant strength was represented by aggressive beasts and [[Sithspawn/Legends|sithspawns]]. In the Second Battle of Onderon, the [[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]] faced a number of [[Boma]]s and a [[Drexl]] larva which were controlled by Sith wranglers, who often carried their light repeating blasters or [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]].<ref name="KOTOR II" />

'''Sith Advanced Troopers''' were possibly an early attempt to make better Sith soldiers in the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Some of the more skilled soldiers of their kind, they served on the Star Forge when Revan boarded it. Normally they were armed with [[Ion blaster/Legends|ion blasters]] and standard vibroblades. They wore crimson armor as well.<ref name="KOTOR" />

During the Jedi Civil War, average Sith troopers were armed with [[Sith Assault Gun]]s and [[Sith sniper rifle]]s for ranged combat and well as standard [[Stun baton/Legends|stun batons]] and vibroblades for close-quarters combat. All types of Sith troopers were issued vibroblades for close-quarters combat and may have been issued with [[Sith energy shield]]s as well. By the time of the Great Galactic War, Sith troopers were armed with [[TH-17 blaster rifle]]s.<ref name="KOTOR" />

Nonstandard soldiers were typically Sith soldiers who advanced in status or Republic troops of the same class who defected, rather than raw recruits simply placed into their category.<ref name="KOTOR II" />
